course_title: Social and Professional Issues in IT
course_objectives: The objective of this course is to provide the knowledge to handle social, professional and legal issues that arise in the professional working environment.

topics.note: Asked in almost every paper. Specific pioneers rotate: Babbage, Zuse, Von Neumann, Dijkstra, Ada Lovelace.
Hardware history often combined with software history. Generations of computers, IC role asked frequently.

topics.topic: 1.3 History of Software: Programming Languages and Operating Systems
topics.note: Often paired with hardware history. OS history asked as short note in multiple papers.
Asked as short notes and in questions about Internet history.
One specific pioneer asked per paper as short note or dedicated question. Most common: Von Neumann, Zuse, Dijkstra.
Broad topic asked in nearly all papers. Covers positive/negative impacts, four quadrant impacts of IT, IT in education/health/business.

topics.topic: 2.1.1 Impact of Technology on Society and Vice Versa
topics.note: Most frequently asked sub-topic of Unit 2. Technology and social change, implications of IT.
IT and women empowerment, rural development, social equity via technology.

topics.topic: 2.1.3 Health Related Issues for an IT Professional
topics.note: Ergonomics, RMI, psycho-social factors, physical health risks for IT workers.
Covers governance of internet, accuracy vs democracy, censorship and free speech.

topics.topic: 2.2.1 Digital Divide and Bridging the Digital Divide
topics.note: Asked in ALL 20 papers. Gender divide, COVID impact, strategies to minimize, digital divide in Nepal. One of the highest frequency topics.
Internet censorship, right to information vs censorship, accuracy vs democracy. Chinese Golden Shield case study frequently used.
E-governance initiatives in Nepal, barriers, ICT in public service delivery. Asked as short notes and full questions.
Meaning of ethics, morals, etiquette, definition of computer ethics, three ways of organizing ethical issues.
Moral and legal issues in computing, ethical/moral/legal perspective analysis of case studies.
Definition, difference, and examples of descriptive and normative claims in IT context.
Ethical relativism as a tool for ethical analysis, cultural relativism, strengths and weaknesses.
Comparison and contrast of the two theories, application to IT dilemmas. One of the highest frequency topics. Theory of Justice (Rawls) also included.
What are rights, types of rights, does a professional have special rights, rights in the digital age.
Attributes of a profession, engineering and computing as a profession, why engineering is a profession.
Difference between job and occupation, attributes qualifying a profession.
Key attributes qualifying a profession: body of knowledge, formal education, code of ethics, licensing.

topics.topic: 4.1.3 Engineering and Computing as a Profession
topics.note: Why engineering is a profession, responsibilities of ICT professionals, IT professional bodies.
Professional rights, right of conscientious refusal, professional responsibilities.
Asked in ALL papers. Types of whistleblowing, conditions to blow the whistle, preventing whistleblowing, professional conscientious refusal.
Concept of professional code of ethics, code of conduct vs code of practice.

topics.topic: 4.3.1 Code of Ethics of Nepal Engineering Council
topics.note: Five key codes of ethics by NEC, role of NEC in maintaining ethical standards of Nepalese engineers.

topics.topic: 4.3.2 Code of Ethics of IEEE and ACM
topics.note: Core principles of IEEE and ACM codes, applying codes to case studies, central theme comparison.
Netiquette, rules of email/forum behaviour, hacker ethics, responsible internet use.
Liability when software causes harm, who is responsible when software fails.
Software bugs and consequences, Case Study: Therac-25, liability for software failures.
Safety-critical systems, software risk in life-critical systems, managing complexity via decomposition/hierarchy/abstraction. Papers WITHOUT: 2013F, 2012F.
Tension between accuracy and democracy in online information, misinformation, freedom of expression vs responsible publishing.

topics.topic: 5.1.4 Misinterpretation of Information and its Liability
topics.note: Consequences of misinterpreted digital information, disclaimer policies and ethical implications.
Value-centered design, embedding values into software systems.
Ethical considerations in software architecture, functional vs non-functional requirements, addressing user rights in design.
Ethical issues in hardware design, hardware reliability and safety, responsibility of hardware manufacturers.
Software replacing hardware, implications for safety and liability. Stuxnet is a key example.
Responsibility and accountability of users, developers, organizations in computing context.
Responsibility vs accountability distinction, liability chains in software projects.
Definition of personal privacy, why privacy matters, how technology has changed privacy.
Types of privacy risks online — government and consumer information risks.
Government surveillance, citizen privacy, NSA mass surveillance context (Snowden), role of government in protecting information.
Corporate data collection, social networking and privacy risks, online tracking and profiling.
Databases storing personal info, technological strategies to protect privacy, data minimization.
Personal databases, privacy in major databases, technological strategies to protect privacy.
Email interception, encrypted email (Signal/ProtonMail context), best practices for email privacy.
Cookies, web tracking, browser privacy settings, privacy policies of websites.
Role of government, IT professionals and individuals in protecting privacy. Privacy by design, technical tools.

topics.topic: 6.5 Offensive Speech and Censorship in Cyberspace
topics.note: Internet censorship, online pornography and impact on children, Chinese Golden Shield case, Nepal's approach. One of the highest frequency topics.
Definition and value of anonymity online, anonymity vs accountability, anonymous speech.

topics.topic: 7.1 Introduction to Computer Crime and Cyber Crime
topics.note: Definition, computers as tools/targets/storage devices, cybercrime in Nepal, Electronic Transaction Act 2006.
Broad category. Specific types listed in sub-topics below.

topics.topic: 7.2.1 Traditional Computer Crimes and Software Piracy
topics.note: Software piracy, unauthorized copying and distribution, impact on software industry.
Digital forgery, ATM fraud (1.4B yen heist case study), Facebook fraud in Nepal, financial fraud via phishing and scams.
Types of phishing (spear, vishing, smishing), how phishing works, protection against phishing.
Hacking vs cracking, types of hackers (white/black/grey hat), common techniques, legal consequences.
DoS and DDoS attacks, Nepal government website incident (2022F case study).
Unauthorized access to personal data, stalkerware, spyware, data breaches.
Harmful online content, illegal content categories, reporting and takedown mechanisms.
Impact of online pornography on children, revenge pornography, legal framework in Nepal, comparison with international standards.
Types of online harassment, cyberbullying, legal remedies in Nepal.
Cyberstalking, online scam techniques, protection and legal remedies.
Email spam, anti-spam laws and techniques.

topics.topic: 7.2.12 Malicious Programs: Viruses, Worms, Trojan Horses
topics.note: Viruses, worms, trojans, ransomware, info-stealers. Case Study: Stuxnet Worm (2025Sp), CSAM info-stealer (2024Sp). Prevention and anti-malware tools.
Definition, difference from cybercrime, state-sponsored attacks. Stuxnet as cyber warfare example. Measures to counter.
Definition, process, types of digital forensics, role in cyber incident response, tools used.
Broad topic. Definition, types, necessity of IP protection, global issues.
Symbol copyright, when copyright begins, duration, international copyright conventions, software copyright, fair use.
Definition, what can be patented, software patents, patent protection in Nepal, patent vs copyright.
Industrial design protection, design rights vs copyright, Nepal's design protection laws.
Types of trademarks, registration and protection, trademark vs patent vs copyright, infringement.
Definition, difference from copyright and patent, legal protection, trade secret theft, NDAs.

topics.topic: 8.1.6 IPR in Nepal: Copyright Act and Patent, Design and Trademark Act
topics.note: Nepal's Copyright Act, Patent Design and Trademark Act, IPR enforcement, penalties for violations, global IPR issues.
Broad category covering all Nepal-specific IT laws.
Objectives, key provisions, relevance to professionals, gaps and challenges, should Nepal do more on cyber law. One of the highest frequency topics.
RTI Act in Nepal, scope and limitations, RTI vs government censorship, RTI and digital governance.
ETA 2006 Nepal, cybercrime provisions, digital signatures, penalties for computer crimes, need and importance of ETA.

topics.topic: 8.2.4 Secure Password Practices Issued by GoN
topics.note: Nepal government's secure password guidelines, key points, why password security matters.

references: 1. Johnson, D. G., Computer Ethics, Pearson Education Asia, Third Edition, 2001, ISBN: 81-7808-306-X.
2. IT Policies, Laws and Acts of the Government of Nepal. Available at: www.lawcommission.gov.np and www.cca.gov.np
3. Hussain, K. M., and Hussain, D. S., Computers; Technology, Applications, and Social Implications, PHI, New Delhi, ISBN: 81-203-0620-1.
4. Sara Baase, A Gift of Fire: Social, Legal, and Ethical Issues for Computers and the Internet, latest Edition, Prentice Hall
5. Articles collected from various Journals and Periodicals, such as IEEE-Computer, BYTE, ACM Periodicals, etc.
6. IT Policies and Laws of the local government
7. International IT Policies and Laws (Source: ISO, SEI, IEEE, etc.)
